description_long: This is a partially constructed two-story house with a prominent red gable roof featuring multiple intersecting sections and a small dormer. The exterior walls are primarily unfinished brickwork, with some areas showing exposed concrete and structural beams. Large open verandas and balconies are visible, especially on the front and side, supported by white columns. The windows are irregularly placed, with some openings lacking frames or glass, indicating ongoing construction. The house sits on a rectangular plot with a slightly raised foundation and minimal landscaping. The overall design suggests a blend of traditional and modern residential architecture, with spacious outdoor living areas.
description: A two-story unfinished brick house with a complex red gable roof, open verandas, and irregular window placement. The structure features exposed concrete and large balconies.
description_view_0: Front-left view showing the main veranda, exposed brick walls, and intersecting gable roof sections.
description_view_1: Left-rear view highlighting the open balcony, unfinished side walls, and the depth of the roof structure.
description_view_2: Rear view dominated by the large red roof, small dormer, and minimal window openings.
description_view_3: Rear-right view showing the back entrance, unfinished brickwork, and a side balcony.
attribute_entryway_design: Main entryway features a covered veranda with steps, supported by white columns and an overhanging roof section.
